# Spectral constraints on reflection-positive stationary processes

*Standalone note in plain mathematical language. Code:
`code/v6_p27a_prrp_collision_campaign.py` (receipts quoted below).
Local draft — not submitted anywhere.*

## Abstract

A stationary binary process is reflection-positive (RP) about bonds if
the matrices G[u,v] = p(reverse(u) concat v) are positive
semidefinite. We show (1) every bond-RP process admits an operator
representation p(x_1...x_n) = <w, A_{x_1} ... A_{x_n} w> with positive
semidefinite letters A_0, A_1 summing to a contraction fixing w —
equivalently, bond-RP processes are exactly the processes generated by
POVM-type "quantum" realizations; (2) every block operator
A_W = A_{x_1}...A_{x_k} with |W| <= 4 over a binary alphabet has real
nonnegative spectrum, so all short-block run statistics p(W^n) of an
RP process are Hamburger moment sequences — extending the classical
single-letter constraint by three block lengths; (3) empirically,
words of length 5 and 6 in two PSD letters also have real spectra
across extensive random search (relating to the Hillar–Johnson
positivity phenomenon), with the first robust non-real spectra at
length 8; and (4) in a large search over VALID processes (all word
probabilities nonnegative, verified by a sound eigen-residue criterion
to block depth 4000), every complex block eigenvalue stays far inside
the block spectral radius (best ratio 0.205), and every apparent
counterexample at ratio 1 fails validity through an identifiable
mechanism. We conjecture that valid bond-RP processes have peripheral
block spectra consisting of the radius times roots of unity at every
block length — which would remove the only known obstruction to the
conjecture that reflection positivity plus finite Hankel rank implies
a finite (entrywise-nonnegative) hidden-Markov realization.

## 1. Definitions

Stationary process p on {0,1}^Z, word probabilities p(w).
Hankel rank: rank of [p(u concat v)]_{u,v}.
Positive realization: p(w) = pi M_{x_1}...M_{x_n} 1, all data
entrywise >= 0 (a finite hidden-Markov model; equivalently, by
Heller's theorem, a finite invariant polyhedral cone).
Bond reflection positivity: for every finite word family,
G[u, v] = p(reverse(u) concat v) is PSD.

Known obstruction (Fox–Rubin-type; proof via the Frobenius normal
form): if some block diagonal satisfies p(W^n) = rho^n (a +
b cos(n theta + c)) with a > |b| > 0 and theta/2pi irrational, then no
finite positive realization exists: the complex pair rho e^{+-i theta}
would have to be peripheral eigenvalues of a nonnegative matrix, whose
peripheral spectrum is the radius times roots of unity.

## 2. The operator form of bond-RP

**Proposition 1.** p is bond-RP with self-adjoint positive transfer
iff there exist PSD matrices (or operators) A_0, A_1 with
A_0 + A_1 = T, 0 <= T <= 1, T w = w, such that

    p(x_1 ... x_n) = < w , A_{x_1} A_{x_2} ... A_{x_n} w >.

*Proof.* (<=) The reflected Gram is <A_u w, A_v w> with
A_u = A_{u_1}...A_{u_k}: PSD because the A_x are self-adjoint.
Consistency and stationarity follow from sum_x A_x = T and T w = w.
(=>) The Osterwalder–Schrader/GNS construction applied to the
reflected form (Klein–Landau) produces a positive self-adjoint
transfer T and symbol multiplications E_x >= 0 with sum E_x = 1; set
A_x = T^(1/2) E_x T^(1/2) and use T^(-1/2) w = w on the invariant
vector. QED

Reading: bond-RP processes are exactly the processes of finite-rank
POVM-type realizations. The realizability conjecture below is thus a
quantum-versus-classical question: does time-reflection positivity
squeeze the extra expressivity of quantum realizations down to the
classical (nonnegative-matrix) class?

## 3. The block-spectrum theorem

**Theorem 2.** For any bond-RP process and any binary block W with
|W| <= 4, the spectrum of A_W = A_{x_1}...A_{x_k} is real and
nonnegative; hence every diagonal p(W^n) is a Hamburger moment
sequence.

*Proof.* The spectrum of a product of two PSD matrices is real
nonnegative (similarity via square roots; continuity at the singular
boundary), and spectra are invariant under cyclic rotation of a
product. Every binary word of length <= 4 is cyclically equivalent to
a product of at most two grouped PSD factors:
runs <= 2 group directly (A_0^a A_1^b); three runs reduce cyclically
(A_0 A_1 A_0 ~ A_0^2 A_1); the only four-run case is 0101, and
(A_0 A_1)^2 has spectrum equal to the square of the real spectrum of
A_0 A_1. QED

Receipt: max |Im(lambda)| = 0.0e+00 over 400 random PSD pairs times
every binary block of lengths 2-4 (dimensions 3-5).

## 4. The empirical reality map and the validity wall

Beyond the theorem, random search (800 pairs per length, dims 3-6)
over all binary words finds:

```text
   length 5:  all spectra real (max relative imaginary part 0)
   length 6:  all real
   length 7:  first marginal non-reality (2e-5)
   length 8:  robust non-reality (0.255; run pattern (3,3,1,1))
```

Lengths 5-6 relate to the positivity phenomena for words in two
positive definite letters studied by Hillar and Johnson; to our
knowledge the exact boundary is open.

Whether non-real block eigenvalues can reach the spectral RADIUS in a
valid process is the question that matters for realizability. Search:
2500 sampled operator-form processes; 2241 pass a length-12 exhaustive
word-positivity scan; 1882 additionally pass a sound deep criterion
(for every 4-run length-8 block, the diagonal p(W^n), evaluated by
eigen-residues to n = 4000, stays nonnegative). Among these genuine
processes:

```text
   best ratio |lambda_complex| / rho(A_W) = 0.205.
```

Every apparent ratio-1 sample fails the deep criterion through one
mechanism: its top is a PURE complex pair with no real Perron weight
at the radius, so the fitted diagonal a + b cos(n theta + c) has
a - |b| < 0 — the "process" must output negative probabilities. The
positivity that defines a process is precisely what excludes top
rotations.

## 5. Conjectures

**Conjecture A (no rotations at the top).** For every valid bond-RP
process and every block W, the peripheral spectrum of A_W is
rho(A_W) times a set of roots of unity. (Proved for |W| <= 4 by
Theorem 2; consistent with the search at |W| = 8.)

**Conjecture B (realizability).** Bond reflection positivity plus
finite Hankel rank implies a finite positive realization, with the
realization dimension possibly exceeding the rank. (Conjecture A
removes the only known obstruction mechanism; what remains is an
assembly question — the multi-letter analogue of the
dominant-pole positive-realization theorems of Anderson et al.)

## References

- A. Klein and L. J. Landau, J. Funct. Anal. 44, 121 (1981).
- A. Heller, Ann. Math. Statist. 36, 1286 (1965).
- M. Fox and H. Rubin, Ann. Math. Statist. 39 (1968); H. Jaeger,
  Neural Computation 12, 1371 (2000).
- C. J. Hillar and C. R. Johnson, SIAM J. Matrix Anal. Appl. 23
  (2002): eigenvalues of words in two positive definite letters.
- B. D. O. Anderson, M. Deistler, L. Farina, L. Benvenuti, IEEE
  Trans. Circuits Syst. (1996); L. Benvenuti and L. Farina, IEEE
  Trans. Autom. Control 49, 651 (2004).
- F. R. Gantmacher, The Theory of Matrices, vol. II.
```
